I am not used to harsh criticism, but this place deserves an exception. Just bad!!

Tale care with the pictures, this place feels like a work in progress, ill start with the positive: beds are comfy as are the pillows. They did let us do a late check in.

The rooms are very small, and the double bed is a little over a single - not great if travelling in a couple.

We had to request a room change after checking in as our room had no natural light or air flow, the bathroom door had just been painted and the fumes could still be smelt. No curtain on the shower either.

After changing to a room with a small window it was still very tight but bearable.

The area is close to the car mechanics but near the metro to get into the centre.
For a similar price I would recommend getting a flat instead

review_text:
This hotel would not even deserve 1 star, because our stay was catastrophic. We had booked in March, a little more than 2 months in advance, to be sure that we would have a room available. When we arrive at our room, we quickly realize that there are several problems in it; Out of the 4 lights in the room, 3 of them didn't work, the light in the bathroom illuminates so little that we had to take our shower with the curtain open, and even then, the light was extremely low. Our air conditioning didn't seem to work, our toilet was clogged without us even using it, we only had one electrical outlet for the whole room and our great view of the city (as mentioned in the booking), was actually a view of the other rooms, inside the hotel. When we expressed our dissatisfaction to the employees at the reception (it didn't look like the description of the room we had booked at all), we were lent a power strip and assured that they would try to find a solution + that they would contact us. For the air conditionner, they did lend us a remote for it, labelled ''Room 612''. It wasn't our room. BUT they did told us that they were overbooked. (It doesn't make any sens as they lend  us something from an other room) The next morning, still no news, we went to the reception for a follow up (our toilet was still clogged, almost a day after our arrival). We told them our dissatisfaction (again) and that we wanted to know what was going on with their "research". The rude gentleman at the reception started by telling us that the view from the room is not automatically on the Acropolis and that there is the city all around the hotel, so that it was not a problem (the problem is that we see the inside of the hotel) He completely denied that it was mentioned "city view" in the room reservation and did not believe us,  despite being shown the booking page. After a heated exchange, this man told us that it was NORMAL to have a room like this, because we had booked it on a third party site, so 50% cheaper! He also said that the hotel is a "TripAdvisor Recommendation" and that it is a 4-star hotel (clearly he doesn't care about reviews). I can't believe that an employee could talk to his customers like that and have such a condescending tone.
*Breakfast wasn't that good, when there are leftovers, they keep them and reheat them the next day (the omelettes and pastries were hard)*
We will never go back to this hotel again and we do not recommend it to anyone.

Unfortunately it has zero rating.
We entrusted my father with dementia and Parkinson's and limited mobility (he was walking with P before entering this miserable structure) to Iasis so that my mother could rest. In total he stayed there for 3 weeks. The 1st time we visited him he had wounds on his wrists and hands ankles from the binding and 1 pantyhose hanging (with this they bound him) from the wheelchair they brought him (which had no footrests),
After we made a fuss the next few times he had bandages so that the wound could not be seen.
On the last visit we went to see him, he had a fever of 38.4, which we understood was not noticed by any of the supposed nurses.
As a result, they told us that he was allowed to be hospitalized and they threw him to us.
My father was dead for 2.30 hours due to severe dehydration, he had one foot in the grave. They didn't even give him water. The owner of the Spatharis center doesn't even answer the phone or call back.
This structure must be closed.
It is inappropriate. I have an opinion from a public doctor about all this and of course videos and photos.
Result
My father passed away in November 4 months after staph and aspiration caused by lying down because he was constantly tied up. The worst part was that 4 months later he still had the scars on his legs and arms and he left with them.
Shame!!!

review_text:
If you are considering this hotel and have children with you, read this first.

We needed a hotel at a reasonable distance from the airport because we had a long journey ahead of us with our children. So we decided while driving to book a room at this hotel. We needed to sleep 6 hours before we had to leave again.

We arrived at the location late at night and there were people in the streets giving us nasty looks as we tried to find a parking spot. Once we found a place to park, we felt unsafe in the area. The first thing that happens is that a man comes up to my wife and says something in his language while licking his mouth.

When we arrived at the entrance, which is the most beautiful part of the entire hotel, we were greeted by a very pleasant receptionist.

He said that the area is not safe at night. We were given the room key and a control for the air conditioning (which didn't work at first), and were sent up into the world's smallest elevator.

The first thing that greeted us at the room door was the door lock/door handle hanging loosely from the door.

The room had decent beds, but the rest felt very indelicate. The room smelled of sewage, it was extremely worn all over and the bathroom had a cockroach jumping around in the shower.

We heard a lot of noise from other guests. it took time before we were able to sleep and we got maybe 3-4 hours of sleep. We just wanted out and away from this place.

We also parked in the middle of a market that set up on the corner, which locked us in for the morning. Fortunately, there was a nice fellow who helped us out.

There was an incredible amount of queues at 7 o'clock and it took us almost an hour and a half to get to the airport.

We had a stressful and horrible experience.

I booked this hotel so I walk to my daughter's family home and put my head down. If you want to put your head down, maybe it's OK. But if want full hospitality, this is not the place. If you want a drink, they offer a bottle of water for €1 and no extras. Please think what you want from your travels before booking here. The good thing about the hotel, you have many shops and restaurants plus access to transportation.

review_text: The interior of this apartment was nice, which was the only positive. We almost did not get out of the car when we arrived at the apartment because the neighborhood was so seedy and uninviting to say the least. Barred windows and graffiti on the entirety of all buildings surrounding this place. There was a big metal door that we had extreme difficulty locking and unlocking. If we did not have two men with us, we would never have been able to get in and out. No response from the owner when we called about that.  Blue Ark offered to arrange a tour for us through Athens. A driver came to pick our group of 6 up at 8:30 AM as arranged. First  we picked up a woman introduced as our tour guide. We stopped for a few minutes for the changing of the guards at the Parliament building and then the driver took us to the foot of the Acropolis. There the tour guide said we had to go in on our own to get our tickets and then read the plaques as we walked through the Acropolis. She could not enter with us because she forgot her tour guide badge . We then proceeded to walk up the hill and stand on line in the heat for 35 minutes only to find out that entry to the Acropolis is time stamped. So instead of getting into the Acropolis at 9:30 the time that we were dropped off there, we had to call the driver back because our time of entry was not until 11:45 AM. These tickets could have been purchased online and any reputable tour guide would know that and also be aware that there are lines to get the tickets and entry times. When they returned to get us, we requested that we stop for a sandwich because now we were going to be out much longer than anticipated . This was scheduled to be a 4 hour tour and the cost for six of us was €290. By the time we sat in over an hour of traffic to get sandwiches  we had to eat them in the car so that we could get back to the acropolis for our entry at 11:45 a.m. That ended our “tour”.  The driver and tour guide refused to come back to pick us up after we went through the Acropolis on our own because the 4 hour tour time was going to expire at 12:30, before we were out of the Acropolis. We paid them €290 in cash and then had to pay €60 for two cabs back to the apartment. Both Blue Ark and the car service company Fidelis Executive Transfer adamantly refused to provide any compensation to our group for this One Way ride to the Acropolis.

review_text:
My husband and I booked our room more than a month in advance. Due to the strikes, there were an influx of other travelers who made their booking 24 hours or less. Because we were arriving after 20:00 (reception closes then), the hotel sent us a message with our room number. They said our room key would be in the door and the front door would be open. This immediately didn't sit right with me for safety reasons. (Anyone could come in and take a room.) When we arrived, we found someone already in our room. We weren't about to kick her out and get a dirty room, but I also have no idea whether she actually reserved the room because she never showed me proof. However, taking her at her word, we found one room left with the key in it. We decided to take it. About an hour later, we received a knock on our door. A second lady showed me the message from Reception that we were, in fact, in her room. We didn't want to screw over someone else just because our plans were ruined by another traveler and lack of planning from the Reception desk. We ended up giving the lady the room and went straight to the airport without a bed to sleep in.

I could understand if this was a small village and reception closed at 20:00, but this is a capital city. Also, leaving keys and doors unlocked is very unsafe, especially because you can see in the hotel from the street. For boasting about being "tech-forward," this hotel isn't even able to solve a simple problem of checking guests in accurately. I am thoroughly disappointed that I emailed the hotel twice with no response. We received no refund and we didn't even end up staying here.

review_text:
I really enjoyed my stay at Petaluda, it was just right for me. Izabella and Manolis are lovely people and great hosts who gave all directions for arriving, it's around a 15 minute walk from Victoria station. For the budget price you're paying, it's perfect. The place is cosy, charming and clean with access to a shared kitchen and bathroom that were always very clean, I only ran into some guests once with a friendly hello but besides that you have your privacy. The room is totally fine and spacious, and I had my own private balcony with a view over the rooftops and streets. Bed was comfy enough for me!

There are people in other reviews complaining about the area being "dangerous" but I didn't feel this, Athens has it's gritty charm many places and I like that, the local people are fine and friendly but I guess some people are frightened by a little graffiti and diverse communities... Go pay €150 per night in Monistraki if that's a problem for you.

Manolis and Izabella also kindly let me check out an hour later and I really appreciated that, when I need to I'll stay again for sure! Thanks. See you again

review_text:
Zeus Luxury Business Suite
Super clean, easy to reach, comfortable and with the loveliest hosts apartment

The reservation process was uncomplicated, and the hosts were very forthcoming to answer any questions or information prior to our arrival.
A few days before our arrival the hosts created a WhatsApp group chat to facilitate the communication, and arrange the details about their pick-up service.
Getting there: the apartment is both by car or train easy to reach. There is a train station from the M3 line close to it which allows an easy access from the airport and to the main tourist destinations.
Apartment location:  the apartment is in the suburbs, however due to its proximity to the underground station it is easy to reach all major tourist attractions like the Acropolis (around 25 minutes from door to door).
The neighborhood is lively but not loud, and you can observe the locals going about their daily business and get a feeling how it is to live there. Everything you need is around the corner like drugstores, supermarkets, restaurants, cafes, hairdressers ..... everyone is very friendly and do their best to speak English.
Checking in:  before arrival our hosts gave us the pin number to the keys' safe outside the building. Then you go down a flight of stairs ( about 5 steps) and you find the apartment on the ground floor directly by the entrance. When we got in we found a cozy, squeaky clean, modern and pleasantly decorated place. The color of the walls, furniture and textiles are all matched with care and love, and very instagrammable ( if you're into it). Since the apartment is on the ground floor and well protected from sunlight and heat it is pretty cool inside and I can imagine it is really pleasant during summertime. The place is both equipped with radiators and air conditioning.
The check in procedure was pleasant and overall we had an excellent experience with our hosts.
The rooms: everything was spotless, bedsheets were the brightest white and smelled fresh. The overall cleanliness and hygiene of the whole place is excellent.
When you walk in you have a spacious room with the living and dining are as well as hooks to hang the jackets and a built in closet.
Everywhere you find notes with the Wifi password, how to heat the shower's water ...
To the left you can find a small narrow, however fully equipped kitchen. We stayed there for ten days and were perfectly able to prepare our meals. They have a good range of kitchen appliances with a microwave oven and a capsule coffee machine, but no oven.
Straight ahead you can find a small corridor with a spacious bathroom to the left and a spacious cozy bedroom to the right.
The shower has an overhead rain shower and enough room to move and relax. It also includes a washing machine and a dryer.
The bed in the bedroom can be split up into two single beds.
I slept on the couch and it was very comfortable and sturdy which was great for my back.
I had to work there for a few days and managed to do so comfortably in the dining area as well as on the couch. The internet connection was stable and quick ( about 5G).
Altogether we had a great experience with the apartment and in the city. Our hosts with all the information and support they offered played a central role and had a great impact in our time there. We definitely recommend it.
A huge thanks to Michael and Madeline for your kindness and time! We hope to see you again!!!

Where to start? It will be obvious to say that the reason why you book this apartment is clear: the hot tub with a view of the acropolis. Well, if you are also interested in these two things, think twice or thrice before booking here. Because the hydromassage doesn't work and the view is not like in the photo. As for the view, you can see for yourself from the attached photos: do you notice any differences? I do. Someone must have had fun with Photoshop.
Regarding the hot tub, we noticed that at a certain point the tub, although THE WATER LEVEL WAS NORMAL AND NOT ON THE MARGIN, the tub was LEAKING and the apartment was flooding. Furthermore, the bubble system only makes noise, but once immersed you realize that it is particularly weak and the water surface remains intact, smooth, like a lake. In short, you don't enjoy the hydromassage, but only a normal bathtub with background noise. What can I say? Once we called the owner, she didn't even try to listen to our complaints for a second, but immediately denied the evidence and treated us with arrogance and rudeness. I underline that I have never and I mean NEVER had problems with structures on booking, despite the fact that I have done dozens upon dozens. This was my worst experience. But obviously the ownership will be ready to deny this review too, I imagine. However, I remain with a clear conscience. Enjoy Athens, a beautiful city, but don't spend the night here, trust me.

review_text:
The room was cheap. We payed €65 per night but even this felt too much for what we got. The only good thing about this place is that it’s close to the Athens Central Station.

The walls were paper thin. When I say paper I mean single ply see thru tissue paper. The mattress was the worst possible combination of a concrete slab and a cardboard box. The bathroom was useable but tiny and the provided shampoo and soap were astoundingly bad. The room, bathroom, and hotel infrastructure in general felt very dated.

The only good things I have to say is that the staff was friendly, our room was ready immediately, and the place was relatively clean.

Perhaps worst of all, was the rest of the clientele at the Hotel. We were awoken multiple times between 11:30pm and 1am by doors slamming. Not just closing forcefully, but ridiculously loud and hard slams. One after another after another. Then, at 5:45am, we were awoken by a shouting match between the two guests in the room above us. Obviously this aspect is out of the control of the hotel, but it’s something to keep in mind when considering the price of the room.

review_text:
ERASMUS STUDENTS, DO NOT RENT HERE. PAY MORE ELSEWHERE THAN LIVE HERE. IT IS NOT WORTH IT

This place is fine if you don't mind living like in a zoo. If you are a tourist and don't plan to stay for a long time, the apartment is sufficient to spend the night. The wifi is okay at best, but it does not perform very well due to the location. Other living commodities are pretty decent. If you plan to rent the double room, I suggest renting elsewhere for the same price, there are plenty of options. If you plan to rent in the shared bedroom, I suggest you don't. There is usually at least one resident who is ether, like some sort of escaped convict, a very sensitive person, or just a creepy person, so he will get on your nerves. There is no table, no proper storage besides a small locker. Kichen is very small (it's like 4m²) so it can get pretty crowded. The rooms have an ongoing bed bug problem that is not really resolved (it is for all bedrooms). The landlord will tell you that there was a professional cleaning done to remove them, while in reality, it was just his sister and cousin doing just a pretty ordinary cleaning with, I guess little extra steaming. Another thing to note is that the landlord, while being polite and nice, is a bit incompetent. The location is close to the center, so that's a plus. Neibourhood is definitely "real Athens," so you should expect to see dirty streets with crackheads all around.
Overall, not recommended, especially for Erasmus students. Do yourself a favor and pay more for better. This is not worth it.

Be careful with them! (ERasmus Rooms Athens - picture uploaded bellow)...
Writing this as a warning to all my Erasmus colleagues.
They extra charge you for things they don't mention until the signing of the contract (like borrowing/washing towels and linen for 60 euros, cleaning, etc.). Furthermore, you're lucky if you get a fully functioning apartment. Most of them are old, in buildings where neighbours don't really appreciate students and complain/call the police and the apartments themselves are falling apart! This is the biggest issue. They have poorly maintained ours for months, trying to fix the toilets, both of the refrigerators weren't working, it took them almost a month to provide us with wifi and so on.
Don't expect them to refund you for anything, after they kick you out they wont send any word until they give you your deposit back without explanation.

review_text: Please be careful, this company is misleading. I was an Erasmus student in Athens, I lived there for 4 months, in Omonia. First I found the company via internet, everything was fine with them. I paid a deposit in the value of 1 month's rent. Upon arrival, they contacted me immediately to come sign the documents. They state uniform accommodation prices everywhere, but upon arrival they start charging meaningless fees, e.g. for bed linen, cleaning lady (she really cleaned), towels, etc. The room was relatively fine, but during my entire stay they weren’t able to fix a toilet bowl (even though I warned them several times afterwards). The bathroom wasn’t in the best condition. At the end of my stay, I was completely surprised that they didn’t send me the deposit within the agreed time. They sent it to me only after I contacted them, of course later than stated in the contract. I didn't even get half of the agreed amount back. Their justification was additional nonsensical charges for linen, towels, bank charges and they even said they had to specially clean my “dirty” room. When I contacted them again for a refund, they didn’t reply me. I definitely don’t recommend this company for accommodation in Athens, it’s not so cheap and u cannot trust hem. My Erasmus colleagues had much nicer accommodation and even much cheaper, where I spent more time. Take care.

review_text: This place was absolutely amazing, insanely clean even for Canadian standards, spacious, and close to everything. They have a really nice breakfast menu you can order food from the night before. They accommodated us with luggage storage and laundry even though we had already checked out. Reception is available to help 24 hours a day with no blackout periods. The cleaning staff was so nice that they even helped us with the laundry machine when they saw us struggling with it. Building, room, storage and amenity access was all provided through our individual code so there wasn't a need to carry around an extra pair of keys. They made us feel like important guests. review_text: The Athens Zafolia hotel was chosen by my tour group. The hotel is located in the heart of Athens. Very easy to get to all major areas of interest. I couldn't stand to be even in the water during that time without a place to find some shade. Other than that, the breakfast was great. Good selection, great coffee and juice. Hotel staff were very friendly. The rooms and bathrooms could use some updating. Although, they were very clean. For the time we stayed, it was very nice.

review_text: I stayed at the Q Hotel for 4 nights and it was a divine experience. The staff was friendly and helpful - the breakfast was nourishing with a wide variety of sweet & savory options and included a fresh made coffee drink every morning (I suggest the freddo cappuccino!) The neighborhood was quiet and charming, with good street art and even better food. The bed was incredibly comfortable, which was a happy change from my stay on the more rustic islands! The rooftop balcony made my entire trip complete, a perfect place to bring dinner back to or enjoy a bottle of wine with friends. At one point there was an accordion player roaming the streets below as I was enjoying the balcony and it felt like the most magical european experience. The Q is close to the metro, but I opted to walk to & from the city center each day, and never once felt unsafe as a solo woman traveling. 10/10 experience, I will be staying here again!

review_text: We were VERY satisfied with this accommodation. The owner was extremely friendly and helpful both in advance and in the location. The apartment is just a few minutes away by metro from the Acropolis, and you can use the rooftop jacuzzi and sunbeds to watch the Acropolis itself anytime you are "home". That is definitely unrivaled! The area has some bakeries, cafés, restaurants, and a supermarket, so that is great too. Even though a train and traffic come and go by the accommodation, you cannot really hear it; it's very serene inside. The apartment is very clean, spacious, and beautiful. Its only downside is - much like almost everywhere in Greece - that you are not allowed to flush the toilet paper, so it has to go in a bin. But the owners did everything they could to make yourself as comfortable as possible, including a lot of food already in the fridge and on the counters that you can eat for breakfast or whenever. And a very generous amount of toiletries too! 10/10 would go again and I recommend it wholeheartedly!

Mold, Not Clean, AirCon Not Working, Awful!

This has been one of my worst experiences staying at an apartment.

Problems:  Air Condition
I contacted the property letting them know the AC was not working in bedroom on Sunday at 10:30am. They didn’t get back to me until 5PM letting me know they would try to send someone there. I waited there from 5-8:30pm until they told me they couldn’t.  Monday came and then nothing - they didn’t try to fix it, they didn’t try to call me, they didn’t even call the platform I booked it on.
Kitchen
There was black mold in the refrigerator and the kitchen was not prepared - the utensils were broken and there was no garbage to throw things away.
There was also no paper towels to clean things up with.
The dish sponge was old and disgusting.

Remotes:
The batteries were dead in the remotes and that was a challenge trying to turn things on/off.

Area: The hallway was terrible - the smells as other descriptions said was awful.
Because it is street level you can hear all the noises.
When people were coming and going - you could hear the front door slam shut and it would echo through the apartment.

Wifi
The wifi needs to be reset every 4-5 hours and it was slow. Can not handle media calls.

review_text:
I know that the rooms are shared, and I was actually looking forward to meet some fun people, but Iconic had other plans for me. As I entered the room, I was dismayed by the bad odor. The room stinked, to put it nicely. A minute later I understood why, as my roomate-to-be entered. His clothes were torn from head to toe, and he had almost no teeth. A homeless, I presumed. As much as it is a sad case, I was not expecting that Iconic Athens Hostel caters to that kind of guests.

The staff was not eager to accomodate a room change for me, and initially told me that they were completely booked, which a visit to Booking.com quick disproved. After a very long wait, I was finally offered a different room. The card was broken, the bedding had punctures in it, and I have had enough of their poor sanitary conditions and rock bottom standards. I left and got a much better accomodation somewhere else.

When I approached the management suggesting that we leave this unfortunate event behind us by them refunding me what I paid and didn't use, and me deleting this review, the rude lady accused me for blackmailing them... They then went ahead and lied to Booking.com that I actually stayed in the property, so that I would not receive any compensation from Booking.com. Of course I've recorded a video of my self check out, so they're out of luck.

I guess I shouldn't have expected acceptable standards of service at a hostel that is this poorly ran.

As promising as it looks online, I do not recoemmend Iconic Athens Hostel for any traveler, even as a last resort accomodation. Even for free. You might as well sleep in the streets for a similar experience. Thanks for reading, and take care.

Terrible location, unsafe pool.

review_text:
Our stay at Athens Mosaico Suites & Apartments was an absolute fiasco. We've never felt the urge to escape a hotel as urgently as we did upon setting foot in this place. When we asked to cancel our booking and get our money back, the reception showed zero flexibility, effectively trapping us in this dump.

The photos you'll find online are a masterclass in deceit. Crafted from the most misleading angles, they create a false narrative so far removed from the grim reality that it's practically fraudulent. Walking into the actual rooms was a slap in the face—grimy, under-equipped, and wholly disappointing.

Now, let's talk about the so-called 'breakfast.' Forget a hot meal or even a continental spread; all you get are breakfast boxes filled with inedible slop. It's as if they deliberately sourced the worst food imaginable. Imagine eating cardboard—now make that cardboard taste worse, and you'll have a pretty good idea of what to expect.

The rooftop pool? Another massive letdown. It's small, filthy, and the limited seating area is so cramped you’d be better off standing. It’s practically a glorified puddle.

In summary, this has been one of the worst hotel experiences of our lives. If you value your time, money, and general well-being, avoid Athens Mosaico Suites & Apartments like the plague.

review_text:
DO NOT STAY HERE! We booked this hotel via Expedia because of the good reviews. I'm now certain those were all fake reviews. The hotel itself may be nice but it is dirty and in no way a 4 star hotel! It is located in the worst part of Athens, we had cab drivers refuse to take us to the area. There was a car with broken windows right in front of the main door, homeless and drug addicts everywhere. You can't walk around in that area.
The room itself was nice, but as others have said the shower handle would not stay affixed to the wall which made showering a pain.
The power went out the first two nights, which also meant no AC or hot water. However, power was on throughout the rest of the neighborhood.
As far as cleanliness... upon our arrival we found feminine hygiene wrappers, old socks, soiled towels and foreign papers in our room. Two of our pillows didn't have pillow cases and one comforter was heavily stained with what looked to be dried blood. They never once cleaned our room, despite our countless requests to multiple people, and the request to clean sign on our door! Every time we inquired about it we were told they would get to us... never once did they! At checkout the person behind the desk said he wasn't the right person to complain to....
Breakfast is served via a third party, they don't have room services, in brown takeout boxes and didn't really taste good. For coffee we have a coffee machine in the room but no pods... They wanted to charge us 5 euros for a pack of pods.
And for the cherry on top... The pool, which they keep showing in all photos, is a joke, in need of serious repair. The pool lights were dangling on the bottom of the pool, and tiles were missing all over. When we told the front desk about the hazards in the pool area, which by the way only consists of 5 chairs, we were told to not worry as they pop right back on ....
PLEASE avoid this place!

Firstly, you have to remember that this building is located in a very suburban area of the city. Although modern, luxurious it is not.
Personal classification would not be a hotel, more serviced studio apartments.
Clean, functional rooms consisting of open plan bedroom/kitchen with separate toilet and shower room divided by a glass sliding wall.
Everything it provided for your stay and housekeeping comes with fresh towels every other day.
You are located with a residential area with a bar/restaurant opposite that does have live music and a street market every Wednesday.....so if it's peace and quiet your after, then I would suggest elsewhere.
Positives - Easy access to all forms of public transport. Modern feel including keyless entry. Price per night
Negatives - Rooftop bar not used. No dining area as breakfast is brought to your room making it very sterile.
Full time reception facilities unavailable (office hours only)

review_text:
From the moment I stepped into the hotel, I was greeted by Marina and the charming team of ladies at the front desk and kitchen, whose warmth and helpfulness defined my stay. They provided invaluable advice, detailed maps, refreshing drinks, and thoughtful guidance, ensuring every moment was filled with comfort and care.

Upon my early arrival, well before check-in time, I was graciously welcomed with coffee and cake in the serene ambiance of the hotel's open terrace, a tranquil retreat at the back of the property. This unexpected gesture set the tone for the exceptional hospitality that followed.

My spacious room exceeded expectations, offering ample space to effortlessly unpack my two suitcases. The attention to detail was evident, ensuring a seamless and comfortable stay.

During check-in, the staff thoughtfully inquired about my flight time, demonstrating their commitment to personalized service. Their offer to prepare a breakfast box for my early departure exemplified their dedication to guest satisfaction.

While the hotel's location may not be in the most picturesque neighborhood, the convenience of affordable taxi services made exploring Athens effortless. Coupled with the reasonable room rates, the value proposition was undeniable.

In summary, my experience at this hotel surpassed all expectations. The genuine warmth of the staff, coupled with the comfortable accommodations and thoughtful gestures, left a lasting impression. Without a doubt, I will eagerly return on my next visit to Athens.

review_text:
We recently stayed at the Mansion Hotel, and while there were some positive aspects, the overall experience was overshadowed by its location.

First, the good: The service at the Mansion is commendable. The staff and the owner are friendly and accommodating, making sure all your basic needs are met. The hotel itself is newly renovated, which is a nice touch—everything feels fresh, modern, and well-maintained. The rooms, while a bit small, have all the essentials you need for a comfortable stay. Additionally, the hotel is quite affordable, making it a budget-friendly option for travelers.

Unfortunately, the location is a major downside. Despite being near a number of shops, the area surrounding the hotel feels unsafe, especially at night. There were many drunk people loitering outside, and I also noticed individuals engaging in drug use nearby. It's the kind of environment that makes you want to stay inside after dark and avoid venturing out. The hotel itself can only do so much, but the surrounding area is a huge detractor and something to seriously consider before booking.

Overall, while the Mansion Hotel offers good service and clean accommodations, the poor location is a significant issue. If safety and surroundings matter to you, I would recommend looking elsewhere.

review_text:
I recently stayed at this hotel in Athens, and unfortunately, my experience was quite disappointing.

Cons:
The WiFi was extremely slow and practically unusable, which made it difficult to stay connected.
We had to switch rooms because the shower in our first room leaked everywhere. Unfortunately, we had to wait two days for a spare room, and even the second room had a water leak. It seems like the hotel’s plumbing issues are a recurring problem.
There was no reliable hot water, making showering uncomfortable.
The location felt a bit sketchy at night, and I wouldn’t recommend this place for solo travelers.
The hotel is still under construction, so there's no access to advertised amenities like the rooftop or gym, which was a letdown.

Pros:
The hotel is modern with clean rooms, and the housekeeping staff were friendly and efficient.
It’s located close to the metro, which made getting around the city relatively easy.
Overall, despite its modern design and cleanliness, the issues with plumbing, slow WiFi, and the lack of amenities due to ongoing construction really detracted from the stay. I wouldn’t recommend it, especially for solo travelers.

review_text:
You may wonder why I rate my experience as 1/5 or 2/5. To start, the room itself was clean, well-maintained, and equipped with all necessary amenities. I had no issues with the room or the apartment block itself. However, I did have grave reservations about the surrounding area and the handling of my booking.

As a seasoned solo female traveller, safety is a top priority for me. Upon arriving and parking outside the hotel, I noticed that all the nearby shops were boarded up and covered in graffiti. I also encountered a highly intoxicated man walking by and peering into parked cars. This prompted me to bring all my luggage to my room immediately, as I didn't feel comfortable leaving valuables in the car. I later understood why the property recommends using paid parking with security a bit further down the road rather than parking on the street.

I decided to consult my son via video call for a second opinion, and he shared my concerns about the neighbourhood's safety. Consequently, I opted to book another hotel and left the premises. As I drove away, I encountered a group of around 6 people who were highly drug affected and rummaging through the bins, which reinforced my decision to leave.

I reached out to the hotel out of courtesy to inform them about my early check-out due to safety concerns. I acknowledged that I had already checked in and was willing to forgo the first night's fee. However, I inquired about the possibility of a refund for the second night, to which they declined. I wasn’t bothered because that is their prerogative.

What did bother me was the hotel's response, where they asserted that the neighbourhood was safe and mentioned the presence of homeless individuals as commonplace. Furthermore, they reported me as a no-show to Booking.com to prevent me from leaving a review, which I found not only disappointing but also deceitful and makes me wonder whether their review level is an accurate reflection.

Upon speaking with several local residents, I learned that the hotel is situated in an area of Athens that is considered to be unsafe, particularly at night, and not just for women and families but for everyone.

In summary, I have no complaints about the hotel itself. However, due to concerns about the neighbourhood and the hotel's handling of my request and blocking me from writing an honest review, I would downgrade my experience to a 2/5. I would urge potential guests to thoroughly research the surrounding area before booking, as there are other hotels at a similar price point in safer locations just 1-2 kilometers down the road and closer to the city center.

If you READ the Booking.com reviews, their rating might be high but most of the reviews warn people about the safety of the area.

review_text:
TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

review_text:
This was my families 1 visit to Athens, Greece yet,I would recommend for you to read our experience to decide if this hotel is fitting for your visit...
Our first day checkin was fine. Young lady up front was super sweet and helpful. We did not checkin to our room until 2 pm & she allowed us to get coffee. We assumed we could get breakfast too but as we were grabbing breakfast she said it was not included yet since our rooms would be ready in the afternoon, I’m assuming. We were charged €15 for each of the four of us, so €60 for small pick item breakfast.
Next evening, we headed to our room and a painter had painted the doors & had no “wet paint” signs,my moms bag & nice blouse got black paint on the front. we saw the painter putting up a sign AFTER he had painted the elevator. We told him it got on our bag and showed him and he comes up to us with a dirty paint towel and paint thinner to attempt to remove it. We backed away as I knew that would only spread the black.
We then went downstairs to the counter and told the lady up front. She told us to calm down even though there was no screaming or aggressive agitation, just extremely annoyed. She said we could use a towel to take it off, we tried that already and if it came out, we would not have come downstairs. My mother then says “so you would not be upset if this happened to you?” And in a snarky remark, the downstairs lady says, “no actually I wouldn’t. Our people are not like your people. This is not a big deal to us, these things.” The attitude and disrespect she displayed not acknowledging our frustration in YOUR hotel is truly  unacceptable. We then requested the managers name and she gave us one manager’s name of whom would not be there tomorrow. Which of course, is not helpful.
We went back to our room in frustration and disappointment. About 10 minutes later, we got a call to our room that they would like their dry cleaners to attempt to take out the black paint on the backpack and on the shirt. She came to our room 15 minutes after and gave us a bottle of wine and two glasses saying she did not want anybody to be upset or agitated because she saw how frustrated we were. We made it clear how it wasn’t necessarily the item that was damaged, but the place we got it which has sentimental value.
The next day we are going downstairs to get our bag and shirt. We mentioned how we noticed there were still some paint on the bag and my mom wanted it to be paid for as it was a nice shirt. The lady says we need a receipt for the shirt before she could do anything but then she will give us €300 for the shirt. My mom bought the shirt in Florida years ago, she did not have the receipt with her in Athens. Makes sense? No. My grandma said that it was fine, but can they comp us the €60 for breakfast the first day since we did not know we needed to pay for breakfast. They eventually did comp us the €60 for the breakfast the first day after continuous back-and-forth. Not to mention, they have a sign that says Tuesday through Sunday the rooftop bar is open for an extended period of time yet it was closed and when we mentioned it was closed, they defensively snapped back, saying that there were no signs even though the dog was in the table and outside the hotel door.
The point of this review is that the customer service at this hotel is terrible. They are rude, snarky, disrespectful, and dismissive. Yet, it only seemed to be the evening front desk crew as that young lady the first day was super sweet.
It is always important to read reviews and other peoples experiences as I am posting this to share how the hotel handled the situation and how you would feel or react if something similar were to occur. If you don’t care about customer service, the rooms were nice and the beds were comfortable enough. The showers are quite slippery and water splashes out of the shower, but other than that, the rooms are secure. I strongly recommend you continue your search for a hotel in Athens, Greece if this is the one you were initially considering.
Walls are also thin. Can hear everything.

review_text:
This hotel is listed as a 4-star, but it’s far from deserving that rating—closer to a 1-star experience, at best. Breakfast was very disappointing, with extremely limited options. On Sunday, a typically busy breakfast day, many items were already finished by 8 am. Despite several requests, no cut fruits were available, and when croissants ran out at 10 am, staff refused to replenish them, citing the 10:30 closing time as an excuse. Thank god it was my last day here.

The room itself was not up to standard. Bed sheets and pillow covers were visibly stained, which felt unhygienic and disappointing. While the ground staff were helpful, the kitchen staff were quite rude, refusing to assist even after being notified of the empty breakfast items.

Overall, a frustrating experience in such a beautiful city. I wish I’d chosen a different hotel.

review_text: I don't write many reviews but I feel it is my duty to warn fellow travellers.  This area is not safe at all. After the shock of walking through this area we started reading and asking around and we consider ourselves lucky that nothing happend, there are many many stories of people getting robbed, so beware of any false account reviews telling you otherwise.  Hope you stay away from the Omonoia metro station and area, it is full of junkies, homeless people, pickpockets and worse, there is nobody to help if something happens, no police no official.  Please be safe and stay in a safe tourist area.

review_text:
I’ve had the best intentions to rate this newly refurbished house, which business concept of individual studio units seems to taylor to professionals and digital nomads. I’ll attempt for a balanced pros & cons below:

✅ location, 8 mins walk to Omonia or Paneptismo 🚇 touching the central locations and a few restaurants in the closest vicinity
✅ friction free, key less check in, send on booking, which eliminates any need to contact the provider, 9/10 times. I didn’t have any issues with the codes.
✅amenities inside, the size of the room. Missing iron & board but having a smart 📺 hdmi cable if you need it for work
✅ 🛜 👍 I didn’t have issues but I’m also on international roaming

👋 acceptable cleaning. The floors throughout and patches of dirt on bedside table made the place reasonably clean, not spotless. You need to ask to get your room turned around

❌ beware of room 104. If you find out that you’ve been allocated to it, try to change it at all costs. The window is in a lower ground floor, looking out into a closed off space, portrayed in the picture. Feels like staying in catacombs, murky. Street noice 24/7, echoing within like if you had the buzzing right infront of your doorstep
❌ 24/7 noice. You’ll hear everything! This place has a terrible acoustic predispositions, and didn’t spend a dime on a sound proofing would it seem. I hear Neighbours chatting in their room, playing music, everything that goes on(!), mopping of the floor early morning, people going steps up and down. If someone drops something, it’s echoed and vibrates throughout the house. I’m a deep sleeper but not in this place. Not for people who need quiet to sleep.
❌ the 🛌!!! The main reason why I’d not stay again. The bed is very cheap, creaking. The mattress is awful. Very soft, unbearable. Only after a days sleep I’m considering visiting a chiropractor after my 4 nights visit. Paper thin quality pillows providing no support. I’ve had one of the worst sleeping experience in a long time.
Rooms’ bathroom sink has a reverse hot and cold tabs and the kitchen sink is wobbly (needs looking at)

My body feels broken and due to the lack of good night sleep, I’ve a feel of being in a dormitory rather than in a serviced accommodation. I’ll seek all opportunities to be outdoors as a result even-though I planned to spend some time on working from ”home”.
Even with the change of below quality standard bedding arrangement, I’m just not sure how the Limehouse Athens Solonos would tackle the obvious acoustic problem, the house presents to its temporary residents?!

The room was completely dirty. dirty. Dirty. I have never seen a place with so low cleaning standards.
When we arrived the room was a mess, with dirty chips bags all over the place, the sheets were still the ones from the previous costumer (full of hairs, food, crumbs, stains) and the bed was half made half untied. The curtains were torned and completely brown (dirty ! They were “white” on the top). The main problem of The room was in fact the door: it was broken! It would open without key.
The shared bathroom was the most disgusting toilet we have ever been. Full of hairs, insects of all kinds and dust everywhere. The shower...apart from being completely destroyed, we could see the brown marks (dirtiness) on its walls - if you stay in this place don’t forget to bring your own flipflops! Don’t touch the floor!
In the corridor there is a small fridge than can be used, but the interior was  covered in mold.. if you just need to put a bottle of water it should be alright, otherwise I wouldn’t advise.
There is no internet in the house/“hotel”. The rooter is there, but it doesn’t work.

If you want a cheap quick bed, this place is ok. But don’t expect anything else as all the pictures on booking are FAKE.

Be careful with the window, do not open it! We got stung by 5 mosquitos and we ended up with around 20 bites each - and unfortunately one of us is allergic.

Location is good.

review_text:
We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.

review_text: We loved this apartment hotel. It’s a small group of rooms occupying a floor of a building in the Omenia neighborhood of Athens. The owner is gracious, helpful, and welcoming and good with communication. The apartment itself was impeccably clean, modern and luxurious. Many lovely little details made the stay very comfortable for us. The building looks a little unassuming from the outside but was safe and in a perfect section of town. Because it was so central street noise is present but white noise on phone blocked it out well. Don’t hesitate and stay here if they have availability!

review_text: DO NOT STAY HERE! Only gave 1 star because I cant give a 0. Let me explain to you what a bug party is. This hotel isn’t for humans, it’s for cockroaches. I was greeted by 3 cockroaches in the bathroom. Killed them real quick and everything seemed fine. We come back at night and I open the bathroom door and see families of cockroaches scrambling all over the place, varying from small to large. Then in the bedroom a nice 6-7 cockroaches are enjoying their midnight nap and also a few in our bags which were left open while we were gone. If you want to stay with cockroaches this is the place for you. You can easily cuddle up with 20-30 cockroaches if you want to. This hotel should be shutdown. The owners & whoever else manages this hotel are straight up crazy. Don’t do it to yourself.

review_text:
When I checked in I was a bit confused that I didn't have to sign anything. I got my keys, got up to the room and first I recognized that the cleanliness was very bad; on the floor were crumbs or something like that, especially under the bed it didn't seemed to be cleaned quiet a while.
Then the bedposts were a bit damaged so I was able to look into it, where I saw some people left rubbish (plastik, paper and that stuff).
Then there wasn't any welcome card or "please do not disturb" sign or any information about the city or that kind of things..which aren't necessary of course, but always a nice thing to have.

I soon realized that the hotel really has got wifi, but the sad thing about this is that the room which I had was out of the Wifi's reach...and that's concerning because the Wifi was a reason for me to book this hotel.
Also there was just one hanger in the locker and the overall furniture was quiet used and held simple (e.g. there was no chair for the desk).
The good thing is that the air condition was running good and not that loud.

Therefore the living/sleeping room is in my opinion a 2/5.

Continuing with the bath; not that good.
all outflows haven't to be cleaned quiet a long time. There was a random toilet paper roll at the mirror and the sower had some damages that looked filthy, while on one wall there someone had written something on it.
The toilet was looking fine though.

I'd give it a 2,5/5, because, well, it was okay and it had everything that's necessary for a bathroom.

But the one thing I find disquisting is that once you get into the hotel room/ the bath room and turn the light on, there were insects, like small cockroaches, everywhere quickly hiding. Every time I returned to the room I first tried to get rid of all these insects but 1. they hid too fast for me to get them and 2. there were just as many the next day as it had been the day before.

That makes the experience at this hotel quiet bad for me, eventhough the people there were quiet friendly. Also the noise of the street and the near place with a metro station (the location was good) were not to hear so one could sleep good once the insects are out of the mind.
It just seems that this hotel needs to modernize.

I wouldn't recommend it as it is today, which is why I just stayed there three nights instead of the week I planned there.

review_text:
Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

review_text: I stayed here for a school trip in May. Our first night there, bed bugs were crawling on me in the middle of the night. I did not sleep at all. They did nothing when brought to their attention. Unfortunately at the end of the trip, my group had to come back here. They claimed they had cleaned everything. I thought everything as fine until our 3rd day back and I began extremely itchy and was having horrible allergic reactions. It turned out that the bed bugs were not gone, and I had more than 30 bites all over my body that continued popping up for days after I came home. I was not the only one in my group that experienced this either. We were scattered throughout the hostel both times we encountered them. The customer service was awful, and clearly nothing was done as there were several days in between our stays. This hostel is also in a gross, run down part of down that is a long walk to the city center. The showers are so small you barely fit, regardless of how little you are, and the garbage in the commons was always over flowing. Do yourself a favor, avoid the bedbugs, and stay elsewhere!

review_text:
I booked a double room through Booking.com, but the experience was extremely disappointing. The room was incredibly small, with barely enough space to move around. We had two backpacks, and the only place to put them was on the floor as there was no other available storage. The soundproofing was terrible—you could clearly hear conversations from the room next door, which made it difficult to rest. The kitchen wasn’t even in the same building; we had to walk over to a neighboring hotel just to use it. To make matters worse, the provided hairdryer was broken. The hotel itself was hard to recognize from the outside, as the exterior didn’t look like a typical hotel at all, and the surrounding area felt desolate, with people asking for money nearby.

After leaving a negative review on Booking.com, the hotel falsely claimed we didn’t show up for our reservation and charged us a no-show fee. This is highly dishonest, and I wouldn’t recommend staying here to anyone.

review_text: Our stay at The Monument Hotel in Athens was incredible.  From the moment we booked our stay several weeks ahead of our visit to Athens, the staff was so accommodating.  They assisted us in arranging transfer from  and back to the airport, they helped us identify multiple local restaurants to try, and were a wealth of knowledge.  Every single person on staff that we came in contact with were so kind and accommodating.  The hotel itself is also very nice.  It is an old residence that was designed/built by a famous architect and the historical/architectural aspects were fascinating and beautiful.  There are only 9 rooms, but this allows for the remarkably high service levels.  We had breakfast on our terrace each morning we were there and enjoyed and impromptu massage in the spa that the staff helped us coordinate at the last minute when we had a free afternoon.  Check out The Monument Hotel in Athens.  You’ll be glad that you did.

Update to the owner: No, shame on you. **Location:**
This was the worst experience of our lives. The hotel is not accessible by cab or taxi; you have to be dropped off and navigate through narrow streets with shady people around.

**Check-in Process:**
The check-in process was terrible. They require you to upload personal information and then send you a code to open a key box on the day of arrival. If you don't receive the code, you're out of luck. There are no staff to assist you; everything is handled through a chat bot.

**The Property:**
The rooms were extremely dirty, and the elevators shook and rattled while going up. If they break down, you won't find help anytime soon in that part of the city.

**The Room:**
These were the dirtiest rooms I have ever seen. The towels had an awful smell, the sheets were stained, and the room had a bad odor. It was unclear when it was last cleaned; the bathrooms were dirty and smelly.

We could hardly stay an hour before booking another hotel and leaving. We pleaded for a refund, but the property manager was rude. I beg you to explore better options. review_text:
If this is modern, then I'm definitely NOT a modernist. It's the sort of place that would have seemed cutting edge 30 years ago back when The Royalton was all the rage. This imitation is done on the cheap, with low slung and uncomfortable lobby furniture which is a few years past its prime and blackout drapery which is too short to actually serve its function and worn enough that strands hanging from it. I ? other examples include avoiding closets by having exposed clothes racks, and further cheapening the experience by use of those anti-theft coat hangers so well known in 1970s motels. Shower with non-functioning drain and poorly functioning controls required extra banging to get the right nozzle to work. Most amazing,and I think unique in my experiences of hotels, was a bedside lamp unreachable from the bedside. When I commented on this to the front desk attendant, he was quite aware of the situation and even offered the reason-- "it's the size of the bed. It's different from what the room was made for." Given that this issue is so commonly known that the staff have what they see as a reasonable answer for it, I'm curious about ownership not spending €30 for a functional bedside lamp.
On the plus side, the team that works here is terrific without exception. The breakfast provided is more than generous and the rooftop terrace is a real find.

review_text:
1) Flat is located two stations from the city centre. Very quiet and safe area. 5-7 minutes walk to the metro station. About 40 minutes walk to the Acropolis. There are several bakeries in the area where you can have breakfast and a few shops and pubs.
2) Cleanliness - The apartment was very clean and fully equipped with cosmetics and cleaning products. Also slippers, bathrobes, towels.
3) Standard of the apartment - very high - newly renovated. Manual air conditioning in each room. Washing machine, dishwasher, hairdryer, iron available. Fully equipped kitchen.
4) Host - I have never met a host like this. Panos gave us lots of tips about Athens. He even helped us find a real Greek restaurant with Greek music late at night during our trip. We felt very well looked after. He was totally helpful with a great sense of humour. If you are looking for specific attraction (as concert, sports game, restaurant etc) in Athens, he will definitely help you.
Best regards and we will come back for sure!

review_text:
Firstly,  thepositive here is the location. So close to Monastiraki with all the shops, bars & tavernas very close by. The owner agreed entry at an earlier time which was great.

We found that our room was on 5th floor of a Hotel where we had stayed many times before so we were looking forward to this. We did not realise that our room was an air bnb room within the building & NOT actually part of the hotel at this time.

Annoyingly we paid extra to have a one bedroom apartment. What we actually got was an open plan room with a tiny shower room with a toilet which was not what we paid for.

The quality of the fittings were extremely poor.  Badly fitting kitchen units that look shoddy. Some mirrors on the mirrored wardrobes were cracked & broken.

The curtains were not even wide enough to cover the window so the room was bright at night. The windows do not block out any noise at night even on the 5th floor.

The shower is small & there is a dangerously low corner in the shower itself that could easily cut your face or head. The shower head itself had sharp edges on it as the silver covering was peeling off in places.  The bathroom is so small that there is hardly any room to use the toilet comfortably either sitting or standing.

The kettle is fixed into position above head height which is dangerous & we did not use this. The fridge handle had been broken & was stuck with glue & looks terrible.  The bed was very low not what we expected for a premium rate room, which had chunks of veneer missing in places.

The owner had left an old bathroom sink in one of the overhead wardrobes & a box of tiles in another one.

The photos on the advert we saw do not match the photos that we have taken even though the room is the same, they do not show the faults.

Overall, I would not stay here again.

On balance, I would definitely recommend this hotel.

The rooms are actually a great size for the price and location. I did not expect the amount of space. Everything in the room is well maintained and works. The "mood" light above the desk is an interesting choice. But it is overall very comfortable.

There are three caveats.

First, the included breakfast is just OK. It's also a bit difficult to get to before having your first cup of coffee even with instruction: go to the main hotel in Monastiraki square, take the elevator to the top, go out, turn right and walk all the way to the end past the bar, turn right again and go down two flights of stairs, then enter the room. It is offered 7-10:30am which gives more flexibility than most places. The view is also excellent despite not being on the roof.

Second, the WiFi on floor 5 was spotty at best. If you don't have data on your phone or need to get serious work done, plan accordingly.

Third, full disclosure, there is apparently a club nearby, and you can hear the bass at night. It was fine for me, but my wife is a light sleeper. Earplugs would be well-advised if you are also. Obviously there's not much the hotel could do about this within reason and the laws of physics.

The room was beautifully designed, and came with a convenient coffee maker and also a very nice surprise of a bottle of wine. The bed was wonderfully comfortable, ensuring I was well-rested for my adventures around the city.

This hotel is superb choice for anyone looking to stay in Athens.

review_text: What a wonderful experience. Our first time to Athens. Let’s start with service. The staff of the hotel were very professional and accommodating. We had an early flight out and they were able to arrange a car to the airport, and without us asking provided a wonderful packaged breakfast that was a delight. Breakfast is offered in the stunning, modern, breakfast room or delivered to your room if you order in advance ( which we did). The breakfast was fresh, beautiful, vibrant, and delicious. On to the facility. The space is comfortable, and sexy with a chic artsy vibe. Exposed concrete structure with tasteful vintage accents that didn’t compromise functionality. I can’t recommend strongly enough.

I’m not sure what’s happening with these other reviews or if we got the one horrible room in this hotel, but there is nothing about the photos that resembles the current state of our room or stairwell. We thought we were paying a premium for staying at a charming boutique hotel, but we were sorely disappointed. The room furnishings and bathroom were in horrible condition. Everything seemed old and dirty, and there was mold in the bathroom, which was poorly designed and outdated. The shower is in the same space as the toilet (you have to open the shower door to use the toilet) and the shower walls were crumbling, leading to a large black moldy area. There was a random trundle bed taking up half the room, which we were told was put there because people frequently sleep more than two in the room. We did not request it and it only further ruined the atmosphere. There were only two very flat deflated pillows on the bed. The curtains were again old and we were wary of having to touch them. The white couch pictured in the photos was also in bad shape and stained. There was a random janky TV hanging an inch over the couch and the remote was broken. we really don’t understand how these people get away with charging close to $300 per night. There are so many nicer places to stay for less in Athens.

2 stars for: There was a small balcony with a distant view of the acropolis, which was nice, but there are no chairs on the balcony to enjoy the view. The hotel is located on an incredibly busy street with countless restaurants, but despite being light sleepers, this was not a problem. It seemed that there was decent soundproofing. The staff was also very pleasant.

Do NOT book this hotel if you like peace and quiet and prefer to sleep before 2am.  A bar that plays loud music until at least 1am (longer on weekends) is located _directly_ next to hotel. The hotel's windows are not soundproof (or very bad quality). And because of the small street and buildings there's no other way for the sound to escape.

My naïve question to the service desk at around 11pm if the level of noise is normal and if there is anything they can do (like give me a different room), got the non-ironic answer "well, there are earplugs available in your room".

Would be quite a nice stay if they invested in better windows.  Good central location, except if you want to arrive by car - no parking space directly near the hotel. There is a(n expensive) parking garage near by, but good luck getting there when you're at the hotel and it's busy traffic in those one-way streets.

And a final bad experience: they had no luggage storage (left?) after checking out.

review_text:
Absolutely disappointing experience! I would never have booked this place if I had read the reviews properly.

Edit Added 27/09/2024 - following reply from owner I can confirm that NO contact was made from the owner. The AC definitely did not get cold AND the windows were completely closed. This was Thursday night and the music was certainly loud enough to feel it and hear it all night.

Firstly, the noise is unbearable. There’s a club right across the street, and the party spills out onto the road after dark with loud music playing all night long. Despite several attempts to contact the apartment owner for advice, we received no response and ended up having to find alternative accommodation.

The air conditioning is also a major issue; it doesn’t get cold and just blows out room-temperature air no matter what setting it’s on.

Another inconvenience is the keys being soldered onto a key ring, so you can’t go out separately if you’re staying with others. Additionally, the towels had a horrible smell, like they’d been left wet for days—definitely not pleasant to use.

Overall, this was one of the worst places we’ve stayed. It’s definitely not a “family” apartment as advertised on WebJet. I would strongly advise against staying here unless you’re looking to party all night and sleep in an overheated, uncomfortable apartment.

review_text:
I rarely write reviews, but felt compelled to as the front desk staff really need to up their game. My partner and I stayed for a night recently and upon arrival the first question the receptionist asked was what time we were checking out the next day. I was slightly thrown by this but didn't take offence though am increasingly irritated that high end hotels expect an 11am check out, and not midday. But more on this later.

Our flight had been delayed so we arrived at the hotel at about 3pm hungry for lunch. We asked the receptionist where we might eat and she said that the restaurant on the 8th floor was full. We said OK, but can we maybe get a snack at the pool bar. She also said that wasn't possible. Rather puzzled we went to our room and then decided to explore. Upon entering the pool area we saw an operational kitchen and were greeted by friendly staff and duly had a pleasant bite to eat, confused that the receptionist had tried to put us off going!

The next morning at 10.30am as we were getting ready to leave our room there was a ring on the doorbell obviously to chivy us along. But not only that a few minutes later the phone also rang (with nobody at the other end when I picked up). I could understand it if it was after 11am but I really take issue with this sort of behaviour in any hotel let alone one that is notionally the best in Athens.

As a hotel it's OK, nothing particularly special. We moved onto the Four Seasons on the coast which is in a completely different league and I would thoroughly recommend.

This hotel is not good! The facility itself is basic but fine but the way it is run leaves much to be desired. Seeing all the positive reviews makes me think that they must have changed management recently and the new management is not very professional.

I had significant problems checking in (there is no regular front desk and the building itself is locked) and I was not able to get info from management on the day of my check in about how I would be able to get into the building and into my room, despite confirming with them earlier that they would be in touch with the info that day, and despite my having reached out by email, texts, and calls on my check-in day. In the end I was able to get in okay, after booking it to arrive during the one-hour window that a staff member was supposed to be present and some awkward waiting around with them while they also had trouble getting the internal details from management about my reservation. (Someone who seemed to be a random neighbor who lived there was the one who was able to get a hold of the management in the end.) However, I was worried the whole time during my stay that I might accidentally lock myself out of my room (the door locks automatically if it closes) because I had no idea how I would get a hold of someone to let me back in.

Another totally sketchy thing is that, after I checked out, the hotel  changed my reservation on Expedia (where I had booked) to say that I had cancelled my reservation. I think they did this because they knew I had problems during my stay and this was how they could block me from leaving a bad review on the site! Yikes.

Amateur hour...You're better off staying in a proper hotel.

Our stay at SV Acropolis Residence, hosted by Andreas, was absolutely phenomenal! From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth and hospitality that made us feel right at home.

review_text: The property has 4 rooms, a shared kitchen and a very nice rooftop  terrace with a great view of the Acropolis. There are some steep stairs to climb, so be careful if you have a lot of luggage. Check-in and check-out is done on your own and is very easy. The only downside to this accommodation is the street noise at night, but that's part of the location.

review_text: Don’t let the charming decor of the rooms and the great location fool you. You will undoubtedly receive better service elsewhere. Be prepared before you arrive and expect to wait around with your bags for several hours. We arrived at 7am with bags in tow and could not figure out where the front door of the hotel was. The hotel has a detached restaurant with the same name, and neither are very obviously a hotel entrance. The actual entrance is down the alley, with the green door. But this door is only accessible with a code, and there is no actual reception area, so don’t bother knocking. The “reception” is at the restaurant (I guess), but even though they say reception opens at 8am, the manager didn’t show up until after 10am. Thankfully, the restaurant opened at 8am and one of the waiters begrudgingly attempted to call the manager, who didn’t bother to answer. The waiter was also kind enough to store our bags in the restaurant basement for us, albeit with a sour look on his face at having to interrupt his morning work routine for us and another girl in the same predicament. I don’t blame him; I’d be pissed off too if the hotel manager left me to deal with his tired, confused and annoyed guests. After we finally were able to check in, the manager carried our bags up four flights of stairs for us, since there is no elevator. The room itself is great, spacious and decorated very cute with cheaply made furniture that gives it a falsely fancy feeling. The only downside to the room is that the mattress is hard as a rock. The room comes with a complimentary bottle of wine, free breakfast from 8-11, and 30% off other meals at their restaurant, although I never saw anyone else eat there during my stay. My final complaint is that they have three large goldfish sitting in a vase that is appallingly too small for the species, with no filter or enrichment. I would urge the management to do some research on proper goldfish care and upgrade these poor fish’s environment, as this is outright animal abuse and negligence. I would not return to this hotel for the goldfish alone.

review_text:
Niki Athens was a wonderful introduction to Greece for me and my husband’s honeymoon. The front desk attendants are very nice and gave us all the details we needed for our stay at their hotel. The price point is great, we stayed in a typical room with a king bed and it was much more affordable than those on the nearby islands. The room is equipped with vegan toiletries and we even had a tray of pound cakes waiting for us when we arrived. The rooms are very cozy but felt perfect for just the two of us.
The hotel is cozy all around, it feels personal with a turn key and much smaller number of rooms than a traditional American hotel.
We chose this hotel specifically because we wanted to have the safety net of a vegan restaurant attached to the hotel. Unfortunately, the restaurant is not open on Mondays and Tuesdays, which was a majority of our stay, but we were delighted that next door is a vegan bakery, and across the street there is a vegetarian restaurant next door to a health food store, which offered food security (although we didn’t need it! Athens has soooo many vegan options! I was never worried about finding my next meal!)
Not only is food easily walkable, but we felt very lucky to have chosen a hotel so close to literally everything we wanted to see! The acropolis museum was maybe a 20 minute walk, restaurants we were interested in trying were never more than 15 minutes away by foot, and a taxi is always close by for the longer excursions.
When we finally got to try the restaurant, it was delicious!!!
The housekeeping staff is very pleasant and detail oriented, our room was perfect every afternoon when we came back from exploring.
Niki Athens was extremely good to us when we asked for anything. We are happy with our stay and will absolutely stay again when we come back!

review_text:
A tricky one this.... we stayed in the ground floor, rear flat, the cheapest one. We were after value, as the plan was to spend most of the day out sightseeing.

First, the things the flat does well:
Immaculately clean.
Comfortable bed.
Air-con works well.
Secure.
Shower works well.

The down-side is:
Immediate location is unsuitable for anyone with any mobility issues (steps in all directions), and is a bit run down.
The flat has one window in the bedroom, none in the bathroom, kitchen or second room. There is nothing the owner can really do about this, but it meant the place felt a bit dark at times.
Dodgy wiring and plumbing - everything worked, but there were plenty of weird looking botch jobs in strange places.

It served its purpose, but I cannot help but think that if I had to spend more than a few hours awake in it, it would quickly become unpleasant.

We had a reservation for September 2023.
