However, they said in a very rude and arrogant way that this would not happen. They took all the money and it was not refunded. A new date was not offered.
Money is not everything in life. Sometimes you need to show your human side. I never liked the zero understanding approach to an important issue like illness. People and companies with this understanding should not survive. Therefore, you should definitely avoid establishing a commercial relationship with this hotel.

The location is far from the main attractions. Metro is 20 minutes walk. By metro  the way to center takes about 30 minutes. The bus is nearby it takes about 30 minutes to the center. Taxi one way costs 10-15 euros. So its better to choose other place to stay if  the main goal in Athens is to visit main attractions.
Hot water  is not convenient. Every time before taking a hot shower, you need to turn on the water heater, after 20 minutes the water will be ready (it will turn off later automatically). When you return tired to the apartment, you won’t be able to immediately take a shower.
There is no administration in the building. Communication is remotely, via messengers. The administration do not always answer sometimes for 6 hours of waiting or ignore you and  have to ask twice.
Probably due to the lack of supervision, apartments are often rented for parties. And in the apartments for two guests, stay companies for 10-15 people. There is no soundproofing and it is not possible to sleep in neighboring apartments course of loud music and drunken screams until 6 am.
Good for night parties, bad for usual stay.

review_text:
Staying at “The Edge” has been quite appalling. All looks but no substance. The building looks newly built and apartment is spacious. That’s where the good part of this ends. The apartment is far away from any public transportation unless you are willing to walk for a good 20 minutes through a rundown area or along the busy highway linking Piraeus to central Athens while being dotted by all kinds of strip clubs and other adult entertainment venues. Getting to the Acropolis will on average take about 35 minutes and Syntagama Square around 45 minutes. This is not convenient to most travelers. Had to rely on calling cabs using Uber and FreeNow which both would have an average wait time of 10-15 minutes before any driver showing up due to the cab drivers deciding to cancel for much more lucrative drives after accepting the booking already.

Managed by “My Greek Vacations” and their point of contact Liana felt like there was no control over anything. Liana was supposed to be in touch a week before arrival with all the details of the apartment and check-in procedure. Of course, she never got in touch with such details in time that would’ve allowed me free cancellation. Instead, waited until I contacted her the day before checking in to ask about the info.

To my surprise, I was given a basement apartment which had a grand view in their garage with easy access to anybody off the street as the garage doesn’t even have a gate. Any passerby could easily look into the apartment unless the blinds are closed shut the whole time. When I asked why was it not advertised as a basement apartment with no possibility to make use of windows, sunlight or anything you would expect during your vacation stay I was given the silent treatment. There was no advertising anywhere about them having basement apartments to start with nor was she willing to even answer a simple question.

Basement apartments should be advertised as they would fetch a lower price point and some client may want that, I definitely don’t and would’ve happily paid more for something else somewhere else. The apartment I booked was neither the same number of bedrooms nor had a mention of being in the basement. There’s no way of cracking a window for ventilation so had to rely on air-conditioning which is not something I enjoy having anywhere. The dismissive behaviour from Liana is something to be taught as the worst form of examples in customer service. Ignoring your client until 2 days into their stay saying this is what we had available for what you booked and trying to blame me for making the booking.

The furniture in the apartment, while pretty looking, is low end. Candy for electrical appliances for example don’t scream luxury or standard. The cleaning of the apartment left more to be desired with crumbs on the kitchen counter, old ticket stubs and vape filters left behind from previous tenant. Not to mention the amount of dust and trash under the bed which I saw thanks to coin I dropped and had to pick up. The hot water supply would last enough for a 10 minutes shower, anything beyond the 10 minutes and you are looking at an ice-cold continuation of your shower. Between each person showering in a 2 bed-room apartment for 4 adults, one would have to wait for about an hour between each and every single person for the tiny boiler to heat up enough water for a shower for 1 person. Internet was good most of the time.

I am very familiar with Athens and visit quite often, there are plenty of much better places offering a premium product for comparable prices in much better locations with better security measures in place. review_text:
I have mixed feelings about my stay at this hotel. On the positive side, the staff was friendly and welcoming, which helped improve our experience. However, several issues left us disappointed. First, the pictures on the website were misleading. While the metropolis was visible from the rooftop, the location itself wasn’t as romantic as it seemed online.

The room size was another drawback—they were really, really small. While that might not have been a huge issue, the cleanliness definitely was. There was noticeable fungus in the bathroom, which made us uncomfortable. We were only staying for three nights, so we didn’t focus on it much at first.

What really disturbed us happened on the last day. We had traveled all the way from the U.S. and were exhausted when we first arrived, so we didn’t check if the sheets were clean. During our stay, we spent most days exploring Athens and only realized a major issue on the final morning. As we were packing, we found bloodstains on the side of the bed where my husband had been sleeping. This blood was not ours—my husband checked himself, and I am physically unable to have periods, so it was clearly from a previous guest. It was shocking and unsettling to think my husband had been sleeping on bloodstained sheets for days. When we approached the front desk about it, the receptionist seemed skeptical, almost as if questioning if the blood was ours, which made the situation even more frustrating.

Finding someone else’s blood on our bed was not only gross but also potentially dangerous. It raised concerns about hygiene and safety that shouldn’t be overlooked.

We feel that when we approached the front desk to address what happened, the hotel should have immediately offered a sincere apology. Instead, it felt like they were questioning our account of the situation. At the very least, they should have apologized and offered some kind of compensation, whether a full refund or another gesture to make up for the discomfort. I even considered pushing for a refund, but given how uncomfortable my husband was with the whole experience, we decided to just leave.

No, I don’t recommend this hotel. And, NO! This is not a 4 stars hotel!!!!

review_text: Anthology of Athens has potential. The location is good, some rooms are lovely and the support staff  (especially the ladies at reception, the servers and the head housekeeper) are charming and eager to please. But, and this is not a rant but a plea for improvement. No one seems in charge. We encountered a different manager each day. The result is confusion and a bit of chaos. We were assigned an unsuitable room, (never stay in #102). We requested a change. First we were told none would be available at all during our stay ( our friends on same room type reservation who checked in with us received a larger room on a higher floor), then we were told maybe the last night, then finally we were moved on the second night. The room, while delightful leads to the second  set of concerns, design  and function.  The vanity in the smaller room had no towel rack.  You had a choice to place your toiletries  on the vanity or the towels. Not both. The L shaped  knobs on the shower are flush with the glass. You cannot open the door from inside if you have wet hands. The first floor does not offer the view of the temple  that inspired you to book the room in the first place. Do not open the window or the exhaust from the street will overwhelm. The new room, was lovely, but the sliding closet doors cannot slide if something thick (think bathroom) is hanging inside.  The breakfast room is on the top floor; the kitchen in the basement. Pity you if your order is missing an item, the server has to return to the basement for it. Fast though she may be, your eggs will be eaten or cold by the time the missing item arrives.  In sum, this could be a lovely place if management can organize and if the designers actually stay in the rooms they designed and corrects the many silly flaws. For the sake of all employed  at Anthology, I hope they do.

review_text:
I recently stayed at this hostel in Athens, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. The place was cozy, friendly, and exactly as described online. Most of the staff were kind and helpful, but I had an uncomfortable experience with one employee named George.

George seemed to have a habit of drawing attention to himself, interrupting guests, and engaging in what felt like a strange flirtation with younger female guests. While these behaviors were off-putting, the situation that most affected my rating occurred on my last day.

A girl had fainted in the kitchen, and I stayed to help her until the emergency patrol arrived. George, however, insisted that I leave the room. I understand that the kitchen was small and crowded, so I eventually complied. However, the way he demanded it was disrespectful and abrupt, which made me feel unappreciated despite trying to help.

When I was leaving the hostel, George approached me at the exit door, and we began discussing what had happened. I calmly explained that his attitude might discourage guests from returning. In response, he violently slammed the heavy metal door in my face. While I can understand reacting emotionally in a stressful situation, such violence is inexcusable.

I initially planned to give this hostel a 4-star rating, but due to this incident, I’m giving it 2 stars instead. I hope George takes the time to work on managing his emotions and avoiding such aggressive actions in the future.

I’ve stayed in over a hundred hostels during my travels, and this is the first time I’ve ever experienced something like this.

review_text:
The apartment is immaculate in every way if you are noise tolerant. For me personally (I was traveling without a child), the noise did not interfere with my sleep, but I have St. Petersburg training, and I walked 10 km a day and got tired. And here is probably the noisiest street in the city: many restaurants and bars (life is in full swing from 7 pm to 4 am), cats are fighting, car alarms are blaring. But in the morning there is complete calm.

Otherwise, it is spotlessly clean, fully equipped down to the ironing board, and the hostess promptly answers questions. The TV was not very useful, since it is not a smart TV, alas. The air conditioners are silent and work great. The bed may be a little soft, but you can sleep on the sofa, it is harder.

From a location point of view, it’s ideal, 20 minutes walk to the Acropolis. Within walking distance are several pharmacies, supermarkets, and a second-hand bookstore with literature in different languages.

I definitely recommend it.

review_text:
We recently spent five nights at the Marble House Hotel in Athens, and it was an absolute delight. From the moment we arrived, we were struck by how impeccably clean our room was, and the warm, welcoming nature of the staff made us feel right at home. They went above and beyond, offering us insider tips on the best local restaurants and authentic Greek taverns. Thanks to their fantastic recommendations, we avoided tourist traps and enjoyed delicious meals daily, immersing ourselves in the true flavors of Athens.

The hotel’s location is perfect—nestled in a genuine Athens neighborhood that’s within comfortable walking distance of the city center, yet away from the hustle and bustle. If you’re looking for a cozy, family-run boutique hotel experience and prefer to steer clear of the larger chains, Marble House is the perfect choice. Our stay here was nothing short of wonderful, and we will definitely be returning whenever we find ourselves in Athens again!

Highly recommended!

review_text:
**"Horrible Experience - Stay Away!"**

We stayed here during our honeymoon, and unfortunately, this hotel completely ruined the experience. While check-in was simple enough, it became apparent very quickly that this place cuts corners wherever possible. They give you one complimentary water bottle and expect you to come downstairs to refill it—no additional bottles allowed. The hotel also doesn’t provide washcloths, and if you need an extra towel, you’re forced to pay for it, which is ridiculous.

The rooms are small but decently kept, although it’s clear that they are trying to save money wherever possible—another red flag is the card system to operate the electricity in your room. Despite the price being low upfront, expect to be nickel-and-dimed for everything once you arrive.

To top it off, the staff was downright rude and dismissive. They had no regard for our personal situation and treated us with complete indifference. We tried to explain that we were on our honeymoon and didn’t want to deal with these petty issues, but the staff, including management, just didn’t care. Their attitude was unprofessional and inconsiderate. It’s unbelievable that we had to argue over something as simple as an extra towel. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone—there are much better places where you’ll be treated with respect."

review_text:
This property is in a great location near all the tourist spots.

It is modern, cosy & compact. With all the amenities for our 1 week stay.

Pros & Cons:

Pros:

1. Easy check in & Check out.

2. Close to the metro & main sights are in walking distance.

3. Good hot water, TV etc.

4. It is located in a quiet neighbourhood with a food market on during the week.

5. Friendly neighbourhood.

6. Aircon was great - we didn’t use it much though.

7. Wi-Fi was very fast.

8. The owner seems friendly.

9. They have a cosy balcony not private but it was nice.

Cons:

1. Upon arrival, we noticed our room smelt of cigarette smoke.

Despite it saying ‘no smoking’ on or door.

I’m not sure if this was someone before us, but the entrance to the flats also smell like this. This was the main issue we didn’t like.

* But I don’t think this is down to the owner, but possibly other guests.

2. We had an issue with ants near our sink area as seen in photos. But we were able to deal with this. I think you get these issues in hotter countries?

3. I’m not sure if it was just our room, but it was fairly noisy with people above us and the cleaner in the morning it was fairly loud, but they have to clean the apartments so, that’s that.

4. It was a little dusty with a big cobweb on our ceiling.

Overall, we loved Athens and we liked this apartment.

We would have liked to have some water in our fridge at least after brining our suite-cases up the hill, ( I noticed other people had water from reading other reviews).

However, there was a local supermarket near-by.

Despite the smell of cigarette smoke, it had everything we needed for our stay cooking utensils, microwave, fridge, hob, bathroom & double bed.

Overall, it was okay and we would stay here again, it was a smooth and relaxing check-in & check- out process. Thank you.

review_text:
We stayed in this hotel while visiting Athens but it was a bad idea.
Although we enjoyed the excellent location and the safe neighborhood, we could not imagine the traffic noise would be so loud. The hotel is located on a heavy traffic road. We could hear motorcycles and cars even at 4 am. We did not really get accustomed to such hectic nights.
Also make sure they send you all the instructions including the entrance code before you arrived. We had not received the code upon arrival. As there is no front desk, the online assistance was difficult to reach. We waited outside for more than an hour to try to call somebody for help. No one answered. We finally called the assistance of the reservation platform who tried several times to reach them and a few minutes after they finally called us back.

review_text:
I had a terrible experience after booking this property prior to a cruise which was a big mistake. Our usual hotel was fully booked and this serviced apartment left a lot to be desired.

There was mould on shower head.
Half a roll of toilet paper provided on self check in.
Curtain hanging off rail could not be closed fully.
Only gave the code to the front door after chasing via WhatsApp after reading other bad reviews about the property being unattended. The owner then sent the wrong code with a typo causing delay to accessing key safe after a long journey which was not appreciated.
There is no one present to help you and we were told to help ourselves to another room. But they wouldn't allow us to see the room first. By text message they said if we are taking the other room by default we are agreeing to vacating the first one.
We had to contact the property to get the codes to the front door and key safe. Without mobile data this would of been impossible and a lot of people don't have roaming.
No duvet or comforter provided. The top sheet was so thin I've seen thicker face masks.
The owner told me to go into the basement to obtain more than a half of the toilet roll intially given.
Coffee pod provided but no water.
No hot water on arrival. I was told by the owner by text message that I need to operate the fuse board myself to turn on the electrics as they had been turned off to save money.
The door didn't lock securely.
In the morning we could not leave as the housekeeping was using the only elevator and we had 6 pieces of we could not send down.

You receive an AirB&B service for a hotel price. Would not recommend.

review_text:
In my personal opinion, this is not a good hotel.

I've had a couple of issues during my stay but the highlight is how we could hear every sound that came from the room next door. Whether they were just speaking to each other, or slightly more intimate sounds, this was not a pleasant experience.

When I mentioned this issue to the staff at the front desk, their suggestion was for me to speak to the room next to us (no thanks), or for the front desk to call them and ask them to be quiet. I told them I do not think it's reasonable to demand that person to just be silent for their entire stay, as they were not being purposefully loud. The rooms were badly insulated and shared a wooden door with no soundproofing. We asked to move rooms and were denied to the claim that the hotel is at full capacity. Eventually, the front desk insisted on calling the room next to us and asked them to be quiet, this solved nothing as they were not even that loud in the first place, and the issue was the horrible soundproofing between the rooms.

This went on for the next 2 days and I'm sure it was as uncomfortable to next door room as it was to us. There was no follow up from the hotel to this situation, and they did not offer us to switch rooms even after other rooms became available.
